justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Distributed tracing gap clearly identified as cross-service diagnosis blocker — detailed in observability review and the 3am walkthrough showing engineers must 'Slack the other team at 3am' without trace IDs. (2) 30-second log delay called out as incident response concern, not minor — noted logs are stale during active incidents and delay could grow under load. (3) Stale 18-month runbook explicitly flagged as 'actively dangerous' and escalated. (4) No recovery tooling for stuck orders identified as critical gap requiring manual DB intervention. (5) Multiple specific recommendations: tracing, runbook update, dead-letter/outbox, admin unstick tool, stuck_orders gauge metric. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: The 3am incident walkthrough is exceptional — it transforms abstract gaps into a concrete, minute-by-minute narrative that makes the impact viscerally clear
Correctly distinguishes between detection capability (adequate) and diagnosis speed + recovery capability (insufficient) — this is the exact framing the rubric rewards
Identifies the partial-state corruption risk (side effect executed but transition not committed) which goes beyond the rubric requirements
Recommendations are prioritized into must-haves vs next-iteration, with clear 'why' for each — actionable, not just a checklist
Escalations are routed to specific team members with rationale, showing operational maturity
Identifies additional gaps beyond rubric expectations: missing stuck_orders gauge metric, no circuit breakers, no error_code segmentation on alerts
